specName: Open a spec from its name in the Specs tree

FR-001.title: Clicking a spec's name opens the spec viewer for that spec
tests: src/features/specs/__tests__/specExplorerProvider.test.ts::gives the spec row an open command carrying its spec directory
src/features/spec-viewer/__tests__/showSpec.test.ts::opens a panel for the spec and renders its first document

FR-002.title: A spec with a recorded run lands on the Overview
tests: tests/unit/spec-viewer/showingOverview.spec.ts::lands on the Overview when the spec has durable context

FR-003.title: A spec with no recorded run lands on its first available document
tests: src/features/spec-viewer/__tests__/showSpec.test.ts::falls back to the first document that exists when the spec document is missing
tests/unit/spec-viewer/showingOverview.spec.ts::lands on the document when the spec has no recorded run

FR-004.title: The tree defers to the viewer's landing rule; no duplicated derivation
tests: tests/unit/spec-viewer/showingOverview.spec.ts::honors an explicit reader choice over the landing default

FR-005.title: An already-open panel is revealed, not duplicated
tests: src/features/spec-viewer/__tests__/showSpec.test.ts::reveals the existing panel instead of opening a second one
src/features/spec-viewer/__tests__/showSpec.test.ts::re-renders the existing panel so the viewer re-applies its landing rule

FR-006.title: The row still toggles on click; the chevron browses without opening
tests: src/features/specs/__tests__/specExplorerProvider.test.ts::keeps the spec row collapsible so the chevron still browses

FR-007.title: A spec with no documents opens without error
tests: src/features/spec-viewer/__tests__/showSpec.test.ts::opens a spec with no documents at all without failing

FR-008.title: No command/view/setting ids change; document rows unchanged
tests: src/features/specs/__tests__/specExplorerProvider.test.ts::leaves document rows opening their own document

context: area: Specs tree row construction (spec name row)
area: spec viewer panel open/reveal path
constraint: the viewer owns the Overview-vs-document landing rule — the tree must not re-derive it
intent: Clicking a spec's name in the sidebar opens that spec in the viewer, landing on its Overview when it has one
expectations: No change to how document rows open their own document
No new setting or toggle for the click behavior
No rework of the Overview's content
approach: Add a spec-level command speckit.openSpec that takes the spec directory and calls a new SpecViewerProvider.showSpec(). showSpec opens or reveals the spec's panel through the existing updateContent path; because that path regenerates the panel HTML, the webview's shell state resets and its own showingOverview rule picks the landing view — Overview when the spec has durable context, first available document otherwise. The tree row simply carries the command; it derives nothing.

decisions.decision: New speckit.openSpec command taking the spec DIRECTORY, routed to SpecViewerProvider.showSpec()
decisions.why: viewSpecDocument takes a file and cannot express open-this-spec; a directory entry point keeps document resolution inside the viewer
decisions.rejected: Reusing viewSpecDocument with an anchor file resolved in the tree — that would copy the first-available-document rule into the tree

decisions.decision: No new message or flag for the landing view — the existing showingOverview rule decides it
decisions.why: An extension-driven open regenerates the panel HTML, which resets the webview shell state, so the landing rule already runs; a showOverview message would be a second authority that could contradict a spec with no Overview
decisions.rejected: Posting a resetView/showOverview message from the extension

decisions.decision: The name click opens the viewer AND toggles the row; the chevron stays toggle-only
decisions.why: The tree fires the command and the toggle on the same click and they cannot be separated without dropping the document rows; the built-in Testing view sets the same precedent, and the chevron remains the browse-without-opening path
decisions.rejected: Making the spec row non-collapsible so the click only opens

decisions.decision: Made the viewer's display-document resolver accept no requested type, meaning first-available
decisions.why: Requesting the spec document as an anchor would pin a missing spec.md instead of cascading to the document that exists; an explicit no-preference call keeps the cascade in the viewer where it already lives
decisions.rejected: Resolving an anchor document in the tree and passing it to viewSpecDocument

plan.summary: Spec-level open command routed to a new showSpec() entry point; the viewer keeps sole ownership of the landing decision
plan.key_finding: Every extension-driven open already regenerates the webview HTML, which resets viewerMode — so the Overview landing comes for free from showingOverview, and no new message or duplicated derivation is needed

tasks.summary: 11 tasks: foundational open path (2), tree wiring (1), tests (3), docs + verification (5)

implement.summary: Spec-name rows now open their spec via speckit.openSpec; the viewer picks the landing view (Overview when there is durable context, first available document otherwise)

T001.did: Added SpecViewerProvider.showSpec(specDirectory): creates the panel (requesting spec, so the display-document cascade picks the first available doc) or re-renders + reveals an existing one

T002.did: Registered the speckit.openSpec command, routed to showSpec

T003.did: Spec-name row now carries the speckit.openSpec command with its absolute spec directory; the row stays collapsible

T004.did: Added showSpec tests: opens a panel, falls back to the first existing document, reveals rather than duplicates an existing panel, re-renders so the landing rule re-applies, and opens a document-less spec without error; added createWebviewPanel/ViewColumn to the vscode test mock

T005.did: Added tree-row tests: the spec row carries speckit.openSpec with its spec directory, stays collapsible, and document rows still open their own document

T006.did: Added showingOverview tests proving the viewer's landing rule is the single owner of Overview-vs-document

T007.did: Documented the click behavior in the sidebar reference: name opens the spec on its Overview, chevron browses without opening

T008.did: Documented the spec-level open's landing path in the viewer state reference

T009.did: Updated the Sidebar at a Glance summary with the open-on-click behavior

T010.did: Added the user-facing changelog entry under Unreleased

T011.did: Verified: compile clean, 1483 tests pass across 109 suites, vsix packages
